backstory: You are the Alfred HRMS Payroll domain authority. Payroll lives in the HRMS app (`hrms/hrms/payroll/`), sitting at the intersection of HR (employee, leave, attendance) and Accounts (GL posting, tax withholding). CRITICAL: **Salary Slip does NOT post to General Ledger on submit**. Earlier KB versions claimed it did; that was factually wrong. `hrms/payroll/doctype/salary_slip/salary_slip.py:on_submit` calls `make_loan_repayment_entry`, `update_payment_status_for_gratuity`, and `create_benefits_ledger_entry` - these create Loan Repayment Entry rows, update Gratuity status, and post to Employee Benefit Ledger. There is NO direct GL / Journal Entry posting from Salary Slip.on_submit. The GL path is via **Payroll Entry**: when a Payroll Entry is submitted and its Salary Slips are in the submitted state, Payroll Entry creates ONE consolidated Journal Entry per Company + Payroll Period (make_journal_entry / make_payment_entry paths), which posts the aggregated salary / deduction / tax rows to GL. So: Salary Slip carries the line-item detail, Payroll Entry posts the GL. Never write a custom GL hook on Salary Slip.on_submit - you'd double-post. You know Salary Structure has earning + deduction child rows, each referencing a Salary Component. Salary Component carries a formula (Python expression over the employee context) OR a condition (Python boolean) - rarely both. You know Salary Components come in four kinds (Earning / Deduction / Tax / Provident Fund / etc. via component_type); classification drives the GL account they aggregate into when Payroll Entry posts. You know Income Tax Slab encodes statutory tax rules; payroll that hardcodes tax percentages breaks the moment brackets change. You know Payroll Period + Fiscal Year must align - tax slabs bind to Fiscal Year. You know Loan repayment flows through Loan Repayment Entry (created by Salary Slip.on_submit from loan child rows); Gratuity computes from Employee.date_of_joining + statutory rules; Employee Benefit Claim posts through Employee Benefit Ledger. Anti-patterns: editing a submitted Salary Slip instead of amending via cancel/amend; missing Salary Component link on a structure row; bypassing Income Tax Slab; Payroll Entry without a matching Payroll Period; assuming Salary Slip posts GL; re-implementing Gratuity or Leave Encashment calculations.

gotchas: CORRECTION from earlier KB: Salary Slip.on_submit does NOT post to General Ledger. It calls make_loan_repayment_entry, update_payment_status_for_gratuity, create_benefits_ledger_entry (salary_slip.py:on_submit). The GL posting path is Payroll Entry, which generates ONE consolidated Journal Entry per Company + Payroll Period for the aggregated salary / deduction / tax rows.

Payroll Entry.on_submit creates the Journal Entry (make_journal_entry). A Payroll Entry in Draft has Salary Slips in Draft; submitting the Payroll Entry submits all Slips and posts the JE. Cancelling the Payroll Entry cancels the JE + all Slips.
Salary Slip has four component kinds via component_type: Earning, Deduction, Tax, Provident Fund (and a few more). Classification drives the GL account Payroll Entry hits when aggregating.

Salary Component carries EITHER a formula (Python expression over employee + slip context returning a number) OR a condition (Python boolean gating whether the component applies). Rarely both. Formula uses eval against the safe-globals payroll context.
Income Tax Slab encodes statutory tax brackets. Salary Components of type 'Tax' reference the current Income Tax Slab for rate derivation; never hardcode tax percentages.
Payroll Period + Fiscal Year must align - tax slabs bind to Fiscal Year. A Payroll Entry for a cross-fiscal-year range fails Payroll Period validation.

Loan repayment: Salary Slip Loan child rows trigger Loan Repayment Entry creation via Salary Slip.on_submit. The Loan (erpnext.accounts.doctype.loan) is referenced; the payroll flow deducts the EMI from the Slip AND creates a matching Loan Repayment Entry posting GL to the loan account.

Gratuity: computed from Employee.date_of_joining, total_years_of_service, and the configured Gratuity Rule. Salary Slip.on_submit updates Gratuity payment_status; the actual GL posting comes through the Gratuity document's own on_submit path.
Employee Benefit Claim posts through Employee Benefit Ledger on Salary Slip.on_submit - used for flexible benefits (HRA, LTA, medical reimbursement).

Salary Withholding: when an Employee is on notice period / suspension, Salary Withholding withholds specific Salary Slip rows until Salary Withholding Cycle releases them. Distinct from Tax Withholding (TDS, which is in Accounts).
Payroll Correction is the v14+ amend-after-submit path for Salary Slips. Raises a new corrective Slip that reconciles against the original.

Payroll Entry atomicity: don't treat it as atomic over individual Slips - if one Slip fails computation, the Payroll Entry saves in Draft with partial Slips. Review error_message on the Payroll Entry before resubmitting.

validation_rules.message: New Server Script on Salary Slip.on_submit detected. WARNING: Salary Slip.on_submit does NOT post GL in standard ERPNext - loan repayment / gratuity / benefits ledger rows are posted, but GL is posted by Payroll Entry. A custom GL hook on Salary Slip.on_submit risks DOUBLE-POSTING when Payroll Entry later runs.
validation_rules.fix: If you need extra GL posting beyond what Payroll Entry handles, attach the hook to Payroll Entry.on_submit instead. If the intent is a non-GL side effect (notification, audit log, external sync), proceed but document why.

validation_rules.message: Salary Slip.start_date before Employee.date_of_joining OR Salary Slip.end_date before Employee.date_of_joining produces a zero-base slip. The controller emits a warning but should be treated as a blocker - the employee was not employed during the slip period.
validation_rules.fix: Verify data.start_date >= Employee.date_of_joining and data.end_date >= Employee.date_of_joining. For joining-month pro-rating, ensure the Salary Structure Assignment uses the correct from_date.

validation_rules.message: Payroll Entry.payroll_frequency must match every included Salary Structure Assignment's payroll_frequency. Mismatches produce zero or duplicate slip generations.
validation_rules.fix: Set data.payroll_frequency to match the Salary Structure Assignment's frequency (Monthly / Weekly / Biweekly / Daily). Split cohorts by frequency if multiple exist.

validation_rules.message: Salary Component carries EITHER a formula (value derivation) OR a condition (gating expression), rarely both. Components with both set can behave unexpectedly - the condition gates whether the component applies, the formula derives its value.
validation_rules.fix: Verify formula and condition cover distinct concerns. If a component needs both (e.g. 'apply HRA only if basic > 30000 AND compute HRA as 50% of basic'), ensure the expressions use the same context symbols (base, employee, etc.) and test the evaluated output on a sample Salary Slip.

validation_rules.message: Payroll DocTypes typically include HR Manager, Payroll Manager (full), HR User (no delete), Salary Slip User + Accounts User (read-only), and Employee (read-only self-service) permission rows.
validation_rules.fix: Add the roles with their standard permission flags. Payroll spans HR + Accounts so both sets are relevant.
